prompt: One of our webhook endpoints went down last night and instead of backing off, the dispatcher hammered it every 100ms for six hours — 200k requests, their ops team is not amused. The policy is configured for exponential delays capped at 2s with a retry budget, but from the logs every single sleep was exactly the base delay, and the 'endpoint parked after N attempts' path never fired at all. It reproduces in the unit tests here: go test fails on this package. Track it down in backoff.go and fix it; backoff_test.go is off limits. Verify: go test -race -timeout 30s ./...
